# Questions in Livestream 255

Live Stream #255: A Very Good Friday

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=517" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:37</a> | Does Dr. Falk eat kasha? | No. He said he does not like porridge and that it also does not fit his diet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=570" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:30</a> | Does the Bible actually say Joseph dug a canal? | No. He said that idea is being read into the text and tied the canal name to later Islamic renaming, not to Joseph. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=657" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:57</a> | Does Luke 24:21 undermine the Wednesday crucifixion theory? | Yes. He argued the Wednesday view depends on Western counting, whereas biblical day-counting treats part of a day as a day and supports a Friday crucifixion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=984" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:24</a> | What was the schist disc probably used for? | He thought it was most likely an elevated multi-wick oil lamp rather than a rope-making tool. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=1179" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:39</a> | Are demons more active at night? | No. He said they are active whenever they wish and only seem more night-associated from a human perspective. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=1231" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:31</a> | Was anything like the plague of frogs known in ancient Egypt? | Yes, in the sense that frog explosions followed the Nile inundation cycle, though the biblical plague would represent an intensified or extraordinary version. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=1367" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:47</a> | How important is pitch accent? | It depends on the language. He said it is crucial in tonal languages and much less central in others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=1647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:27</a> | Does censorship inhibit a writer's ability to explore the destructive nature of sin? | Yes. He argued censorship can blunt truthful depiction of sin and often operates through industry collusion more than direct state action. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=1800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:00</a> | Can a Christian artist sin through written or drawn work? | Yes. He said art becomes sinful when it glorifies evil rather than exposing or critiquing it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=2136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:36</a> | Do you have any expectations for Artemis 2 and future space exploration? | He was most interested in what had broken on Artemis 2 and wished more live telemetry or beacon information were publicly available. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=2214" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:54</a> | Would you go to space if given the opportunity? | Probably not. He says space is very dangerous. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=2779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:19</a> | Where should someone start if they have never tried whiskey? | He recommended starting with a less challenging entry point, especially an approachable Irish whiskey like Jameson. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=2964" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:24</a> | Isn't Earth beautiful enough to make God's work obvious? | Yes. He agreed and treated the beauty of Earth, especially from space, as a strong pointer to divine wonder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=3059" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:59</a> | If Lazarus had been dead four days, what does that imply for Jesus being dead a much shorter time? | He said the Lazarus account highlights the undeniable reality of death before resurrection and reinforces Christ's unique power to raise the dead, including himself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=3451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:31</a> | After full disclosure of who Jesus is, do people who go to hell effectively choose it knowingly? | He said people are judged according to the disclosure they receive and that those who finally reject God do in that sense choose separation from him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=3871" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:31</a> | How can a stable and prosperous Egypt fit with Nebuchadnezzar's invasion of Ahmose II's Egypt? | He argued the invasion still happened, but Egypt's decentralized power, Greek mercenaries, and the location of royal authority help explain how the regime endured for a time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=4356" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:36</a> | What is your view of Richard Elliott Friedman? | He said he has reservations, especially with Friedman's Levite-centered reconstruction, and does not regard it as the best explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=4867" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:07</a> | Does showing a better way still apply to people outside the faith who have been harmed by false teachers? | He says one can try, but damaged people may reject it; that is why he sees false teachers as so destructive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=5705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:05</a> | Why do many North Americans attribute Egyptian and Maya pyramids to aliens or Nephilim but not European monuments? | He calls this the imagination fallacy: assuming something could not happen merely because one cannot imagine how it happened. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=6122" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:02</a> | Why are demons hostile to humans? | He says demons dislike humans because humans are made in God's image and resemble what demons rebel against. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=6222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:42</a> | What is the meaning of the woman with the moon under her feet in Revelation 12:1? | He rejects astrological and Mariological readings and connects the image to the Old Testament pattern from Joseph's dream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=7213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:13</a> | Does *Final Fantasy* have an anti-Christian worldview? | He says he does not know because he has never played the games. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=7258" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:58</a> | Is it a problem if a Christian is hesitant to drink wine? | No. He says Christians are free not to drink, and no one should judge them either way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=7568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:08</a> | Has a priest ever had to take a nap after finishing leftover communion wine? | Yes. He says it has happened where traditions require the priest to consume remaining consecrated wine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=7670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:50</a> | What literary traps do you often see writers stumble into? | He names revealing too much too early, mishandling plot, head switching, and overdone description or purple prose. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=7985" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:05</a> | What is holy water, and is it real? | He says it is real water that has been consecrated, but he doubts its practical efficacy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=8249" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:29</a> | What is proper anointing oil? | He says the Old Testament gives a spiced olive-oil formula, but most churches now simply use blessed olive oil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=8556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:36</a> | Can a demon communicate through an ancient idol such as Pazuzu? | He says an idol can be used as a focus, but the object itself is not necessary; the issue is opening oneself to demonic contact. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=9008" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:08</a> | Is the Sabbath mandatory? | He distinguishes the existence of Saturday from special Sabbath observance and treats the latter as the real question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=9167" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:47</a> | How did family tombs handle multiple burials close together? | He says most family burials were years apart, and when deaths were close, bodies could be placed apart and bones collected later together. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=9582" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:42</a> | What should someone do if a corrupt pastor or priest expels them for telling the truth? | He says to shake the dust off one's feet, declare the anathema, and move on. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=9656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:56</a> | Should Romans 13 be translated as referring to synagogue leaders rather than authority? | No. He says the Greek points to power or authority and that "synagogue leaders" would read too much into the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=10153" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:13</a> | Why does Genesis call Isaac Abraham's `only son`? | He said Abraham would not have found that confusing because the phrase functions as a special covenantal or designated-son expression, not a denial that Ishmael existed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=10665" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:45</a> | Why did Rachel steal Laban's teraphim in Genesis 31:19? | He treated the theft as bound up with inheritance and household authority, not as a random act. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=11338" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:58</a> | How should angels be depicted in art? | He favored depicting them in ways that emphasize their spiritual otherness rather than defaulting to conventional winged-human imagery in every case. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=12201" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:21</a> | Why do many Jews reject Isaiah 53 as referring to Jesus? | He said the disagreement comes from interpretive tradition and textual framing rather than simple ignorance of the passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=12469" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:49</a> | Did the apostles call themselves Jews or Hebrews? | He said `Jew` is the better self-designation in that first-century setting, while `Hebrew` more often points to language or cultural usage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d8y1Gup3qas?t=12900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:00</a> | Did the ancient Egyptians perceive color differently from us? | No. He said they recognized the same visual world even though their named color categories and symbolic associations were structured differently. |
